speakerProtocol.hook: From the previous idea, the next system pressure point is service decomposition across catalog, search, inventory, cart, orders, payments, and media delivery.
speakerProtocol.teleprompterVO: This slide explains Amazon-Style Commerce System by starting from the real workload, then walking the hot path, then naming the key trade-off.
speakerProtocol.stageDirections: Advance after the hot-path explanation, then reveal the trade-off callout.
speakerProtocol.theDelta: The hidden architectural why is that break a large commerce problem into bounded services with separate storage and scaling profiles.

blocks.title: Core Concept
blocks.bodyText: Break a large commerce problem into bounded services with separate storage and scaling profiles.
items: Catalog -> Search -> Product detail -> Cart -> Order -> Payment
Inventory and pricing feed the hot path
Media sits on CDN-backed blob storage

blocks.title: Hot Path
blocks.bodyText: service decomposition across catalog, search, inventory, cart, orders, payments, and media delivery
items: Define buyer, seller, and admin flows.
Separate read-heavy search from write-heavy transactions.
Keep inventory and orders transactionally safe.

speakerProtocol.hook: From the previous idea, the next system pressure point is persistent connection routing plus durable message fan-out and delivery receipts.
speakerProtocol.teleprompterVO: This slide explains WhatsApp-Style Chat System by starting from the real workload, then walking the hot path, then naming the key trade-off.
speakerProtocol.stageDirections: Advance after the hot-path explanation, then reveal the trade-off callout.
speakerProtocol.theDelta: The hidden architectural why is that separate real-time delivery from durable persistence so mobile chat feels instant without losing messages.

blocks.title: Core Concept
blocks.bodyText: Separate real-time delivery from durable persistence so mobile chat feels instant without losing messages.
items: Sender socket -> Chat service -> Queue/store -> Recipient socket
Media and profile flows are side services

blocks.title: Hot Path
blocks.bodyText: persistent connection routing plus durable message fan-out and delivery receipts
items: Fan out to recipient sessions or offline inbox.

speakerProtocol.hook: From the previous idea, the next system pressure point is channel-agnostic event ingestion with pluggable delivery handlers and retry policies.

speakerProtocol.stageDirections: Advance after the hot-path explanation, then reveal the trade-off callout.
speakerProtocol.theDelta: The hidden architectural why is that use one common event model and route it to specialized handlers per channel.

blocks.title: Core Concept
blocks.bodyText: Use one common event model and route it to specialized handlers per channel.
items: Producer event -> Notification bus -> Channel handler -> Provider -> Status
Retries and DLQ handle transient failures

blocks.bodyText: Separate fast-changing geospatial state from slower transactional trip and payment state.
